sourceTitle: Can’t upgrade to the Plus Plan for students and educators

questions.question: If you are currently subscribed to a paid Notion plan, what is the first action you must take before you can upgrade to the Plus Plan for students and educators?

options.text: Contact Notion Support immediately
options.explanation: While support is an option, the article explicitly states the first troubleshooting step is to downgrade your current plan.

options.text: Downgrade to the Free Plan first
options.explanation: You must downgrade to the Free Plan first in order to see the option to upgrade to the Plus Plan for students and educators.

options.text: Remove all other workspace members
options.explanation: Removing members is a separate step required if you have multiple people, but it is not the first step if you are on a paid plan.

options.text: Change your account email to a .edu address
options.explanation: Changing the email is important, but the prerequisite for seeing the upgrade option is downgrading your current paid plan first.

questions.question: When will the option to upgrade to the Plus Plan for students and educators appear in your workspace settings?

options.text: Immediately after you click the upgrade button
options.explanation: The upgrade option will not appear until you have completed the necessary steps, primarily downgrading your current plan.

options.text: Only after the plan has been fully downgraded to the Free Plan
options.explanation: The article specifies that the upgrade option appears **only after** the plan has been fully downgraded to the Free Plan.

options.text: After 24 hours of being on the Free Plan
options.explanation: There is no waiting period mentioned; the upgrade option appears as soon as the downgrade is complete.

options.text: Once your educational institution is verified by the system
options.explanation: Institution verification happens later if the email doesn't work, but the upgrade option visibility is tied to the plan downgrade status.

questions.question: Why might the option to upgrade not appear even if you have downgraded to the Free Plan?

options.text: Your workspace has too many members
options.explanation: This is a reason for failure, but the text specifically lists the email address as the first reason the option might not appear.

options.text: Your account is not tied to a valid educational email address
options.explanation: The article states that if your account is not tied to a valid educational email, you will not be able to upgrade.

options.text: You are using a mobile device
options.explanation: The article does not mention device compatibility as a blocker for this specific upgrade path.

options.text: Your payment method was declined
options.explanation: Since this is a student plan, payment is usually handled through the educational institution; the issue is usually account configuration.

questions.question: What is the maximum number of members allowed in a workspace using the Plus Plan for individual students and educators?

options.text: Unlimited members
options.explanation: This plan is specifically for individuals, so it does not support unlimited members.

options.text: Up to 10 members
options.explanation: The plan is designed for a single user, not a team.

options.text: Only one member
options.explanation: The plan is for individual students and educators and can only contain one member.

options.text: Exactly 2 members
options.explanation: The limitation is strictly one member, not two.

questions.question: If you have multiple workspace owners or members, what troubleshooting step is required to use the student/educator plan?

options.text: Merge the workspaces into one team account
options.explanation: Merging is not suggested; instead, you need to remove the extra members to fit the individual plan structure.

options.text: Remove any additional admins or members
options.explanation: Since the plan is for individuals, you must remove the extra admins or members to proceed with the upgrade.

options.text: Change the workspace to a team plan first
options.explanation: The goal is to upgrade to the individual Plus Plan, not to switch to a team plan.

options.text: Disable two-factor authentication
options.explanation: Account security settings are not mentioned as the cause for the upgrade failure in this context.

questions.question: Who should you contact if your educational institution is not recognized by Notion's system?

options.text: Your school's IT department
options.explanation: While they might be able to help, the article directs you to contact Notion Support directly.

options.text: Notion Support
options.explanation: The article states that if your institution isn't recognized, you should email Notion Support for assistance.

options.text: The Notion billing department
options.email: This article is about the Plus Plan for Education, which is handled by Support, not standard billing.

options.text: A third-party verification service
options.explanation: Notion handles the verification internally, and you should contact them if it fails.

questions.question: What is the requirement regarding the email address associated with your Notion account?

options.text: It must be a personal Gmail account
options.explanation: The requirement is that the email must be educational, not necessarily personal.

options.text: It must be tied to a valid educational email address
options.explanation: You must ensure your account email is your educational email to be eligible for the upgrade.

options.text: It cannot be changed once set
options.explanation: The article links to instructions on how to change your account email if it is not the correct one.

options.text: It must be a corporate email address ending in .org
options.explanation: The plan is for students and educators, so a valid educational email (often .edu) is required.
